race.name: Spinneys Dubai 92 Cycle Challenge
race.tagline: Middle East's premier mass-participation cycling event and UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier

vitals.location: Expo City Dubai, Al Forsan Park
vitals.location_badge: Expo City Dubai, Al Forsan Park

climate.description: Dubai in mid-February features mild winter weather with cooler mornings ideal for early starts, low precipitation, and potential humidity.

terrain.surface: Fast, largely flat circuit on wide, well-surfaced tarmac roads through Dubai districts with minimal elevation.
features: Fast, largely flat circuit on wide, well-surfaced tarmac roads through Dubai districts with minimal elevation

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 61. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 60.

course_description.character: Fast, flat urban loop through Dubai landmarks like Global Village, Hessa Street, Emirates Hills, DMCC; 2km neutral rollout; traffic-free wide roads.
course_description.signature_challenge: Maintaining group speed on flat tarmac for UCI top 25% qualifier.

logistics.airport: Dubai International Airport (DXB); Al Maktoum International Airport (DWC)
logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels near Expo City like Rove Expo City; easy access.
logistics.transport: Start/finish at Expo City Dubai Al Forsan Park; early 06:10am waves; parking and bag drop available; registration closed for 2026.

history.origin_story: First held in 2010; 16th edition in 2026; annual Dubai staple since inception.
notable_moments: Joined UCI Gran Fondo World Series in 2023

biased_opinion.summary: A slick, fast-paced road gran fondo perfect for qualifiers and personal bests in a luxurious setting, though gravel purists will yawn at the flat tarmac.

final_verdict.one_liner: Premier UAE road event for speed demons chasing UCI spots.
final_verdict.should_you_race: The Spinneys Dubai 92 stands out as a top mass-participation road race with UCI prestige, build-up series, and Dubai glamour, ideal for 19+ qualifiers aiming for Niseko Worlds.

videos.title: WINNING my Biggest CASH Prize On My CHEAPEST BIKE! - UCI Granfondo Dubai
videos.description:
Filmed on Insta360 X4 👉 https://www.insta360.com/sal/x4?utm_term=INR47RE 
Can you win a big race with a cheap bike? On this trip to Dubai I took my lowest spect out bike and joined the Spinneys 92 cycling challenge, part of the UCI Granfondo world series Calendar. The trip was a family holiday and I planned a lot of training. This race was a fun add to the trip! 
Sponsors for this video are Insta360, Yoeleo, Winspace and Optimize. I've got discount codes for these brands right here!:
► Insta360 X4 👉 https://www.insta360.com/sal/x4?utm_term=INR47RE 
► OPTIMIZE chain wax, USE CODE Dubai10 (*edit: code expired) for 10% OFF  👉 https://www.optimize.bike/?ref=jasper 
► WINSPACE CARBON WHEELS/FRAMES, 10% off with code CYCLINGFANATICS 👉 https://www.winspace.cc
► YOELEO BIKE/WHEELS Discount with code cyclingfanatics 👉 https://www.yoeleoeurope.com/ or 👉 https://www.yoeleobike.com/?ref=cycli...

CHANNEL PARTNERS:

► Cycling Fanatics Merch! 👉 http://bit.ly/2MWFimr
►Bont Cycling Shoes, use code JASPERVERKUIJL https://shop.bontcycling.com/?ref=D9F...
► Nova2Ride OSPW 10% off code "cyclingfanatics" 👉 https://bit.ly/Nova2Ride
► Insta360 Camera:  https://www.insta360.com/sal/one_r?in...
►Training plan by Robic, use code ''JASPER'' 👉 https://robic-cycling.com/training-plan
► Find the Music I use here!:👉 https://www.epidemicsound.com/referra...
► ASSIOMA POWER PEDALS 👉 https://bit.ly/41IGH1N 
► EatMyRide (nutrition app) FREE 14day PREMIUM use code ''JASPER2WKS''  👉 https://urlgeni.us/CyclingFanatics


Follow me on social media:

do you need an expensive bike
videos.transcript:
[Music]
Do you really need an expensive bike to
win a race? On my recent trip to Dubai,
I took part in the UCI Grand Fondo with
the cheapest bike I own, a Chinese bike
with the mechanical 105 group set. And
guess what? I won the race. And this is
how.
What's up, guys? What's up, cycling
fanatics? This is the second time that
I'm taking the family to Dubai. It's a
combination of holiday and a training
camp. It's midFebruary where the weather
was still terrible back home and I spent
most of my time on the indoor trainer.
Dubai actually has a whole network of
about 200 km of dedicated cycling path.
Is it straight and flat and boring?
Well, I'm used to that back home and the
weather was so much better. So, for me,
perfect for training. A holiday with a
bike and three kids with all the luggage
is no joke. Mixing training and family
time can be tough, but me and Daisy are
becoming specialists. We stay in town
square right next to the start of the
Aludra cycling track. Flexibility is key
here. I could go for my training in the
morning or in the afternoon, whenever
this would fit, and then I would join
them for the rest of the day. A
coincidence was that the Spinnies Grand
Fondo, the the biggest local amateur
race in Dubai was held at the time of
our trip. So I decided to give that a
go. Even though I would race the UCI
Graphondo, this was not the main goal.
This trip was a big trading block. So a
reliable and worryfree bike was the
priority. So I brought my lowest spec
and cheapest bike I have. Chinese bike
with a mechanical 105 11-speed group
set. However, the Windspace T550 is also
my most arrow frame. And this is a real
high quality race bike, perfect for the
fast, flat roads of Dubai. But I had to
make some key changes to this bike to
make it work. First off, the wheel and
tires. I swapped my favorite high-end
race wheels, the full carbon Loom Megas,
for a cheaper, more basic wheel set, the
Yolio Pro Next. Crazy enough, the Yolios
are even lighter than the Megas at less
than half the price. The main reason for
the Next wheels is that they're using
steel straight spokes, which are easy to
replace in a local bike shop, for
example, when they get damaged in air
travel, a crash, or whatever. Both
wheels have different rim depth, but the
yo-yos are newer. They have a wider
internal rim width, and that would make
my 28C tires sit very nice. By the way,
28 mm is wide enough on these silky
smooth roads of Dubai. In this case,
wider in my opinion would not be faster,
and I was actually running higher
pressure than usual because it was so
smooth. I'm riding the Ftoria Corsa and
not the Ftoria Corsa Pro tires. Also for
the same reason of durability,
reliability, and this case, puncture
resistance. A couple of watts would
never make up for a 3hour walk in the
desert when the tire would fail. This
windspace was built with a Shimano 105
group set. And to be honest, Shimano
105, it is not top performance, but it
actually works great. It's cheap and
reliable. And in this video, I'll show
you that it's very possible to race it.
However, one problem that I had, it came
with a compact crank set. Oh, God.
Please, no. No. So, 3450. That's way too
small for the high speeds in Dubai. So,
I swapped to a 3953 that I pulled off
another bike. And oh, was I happy that I
swapped those gears.
The last thing I did specifically to
this bike was hot waxing the chain with
the optimized degreasing additive. And I
was using their ultrasonic wax cooker.
This completely removes the step of
degasing. And you can actually just put
the brand new chain straight into the
hot wax with this degreasing block. And
the goal to test its durability in
Dubai's dry and sandy conditions and to
see how long it could last without
reapplying any wax. I mean, the claims
are up to 1,000 km. And I was very
reluctant to believe this, that this was
even possible until now. Now, the price
of this bike, like I said, this is kind
of the cheapest bike that I have right
now. We're looking at the T1550 frame at
Windspace. That's only $1,049.
the Zero SL handlebar in 38 cm. So nice
and narrow, 290 USD. And then with the
code cycling fanatics, you take 10% off
and the totals at,25
USD or,62
with free shipping. The wheels from
Yolo, the 50s are 999 USD. With the code
cycling fanatics, that's only €8.99 or
€792.
And then the Shimano 105 group set,
brand new, it's about €550
with some small parts like pedals,
tires, and a saddle. We're looking at
roughly €2700. And that's the bike that
I bring to the most bling bling place in

During our stay, the UAE tour was

We uh do a quick stop at the mosque.
They have nice cold water here. So we've

So, cooling down a little bit before we

very high numbers. I can hear you think

not a bad training day. And it was all
just for the fun of it.
Normally, I would, you know, do some
extra prep on my bike the day before a

still smooth after about 600 km. So, I

over 800 km on this chain, and the chain
is still,
cuz that's super noisy. Chain is still

Hot wax, drip wax, ultrasonic wax

There's a 3hour time difference with the

It's uh 1:00 Dutch time.
Very early. I would go to bed well past

Spinnies 92. First year officially part

everything in front and behind me. And
we starting at the 2020 Expo. We did a

This wasn't very ideal, but I think it
was a box that the organization had to

if there would be one, I definitely want

hour and I'm coasting half of the time.

super easy actually to stay in the

I want to be conserving my energy for

nothing, but I wanted to be able to
check out what's going on.
I think the route of this race is is
pretty much the same every year. So, a
lot of riders have done it multiple
times and and it goes kind of similar
every year. I heard that almost every
year it arrives at the finish in a
pelaton. Sometimes there is a breakaway,
but it gets caught back before this
before the finish. And if it breaks
away, then this was the moment. So, a
lot of other riders know this and they
go to the front. The speed is going to
pick up towards this turn and then
afterwards. So, going through the corner
here. Another Well, it wasn't very
sharp. And then
right here, we're going to put down some
power to stay with the front. Now, one
thing that was going to make a big
difference on a day like this if it
would be windy or not, but we're riding
so early in the morning and every
morning in Dubai, there's no wind and
then in the afternoon the wind starts to
pick up. So, if you would race in the
afternoon and if it would be very windy,
a road like this would actually be
perfect to have it break to to break
away because you go to the side, you put
the guys in the gutter, you put 500
watts for a couple minutes, and then it
breaks into pieces. But without wind,
that's impossible to do because there
will always be a a blob behind you and a
pelaton forming and there's no way to
make that difference. So, it's hard. And
that's what you see right now. We're
going 50k an hour. I'm in the front.
It's one single file line, but it breaks
down again and other guys take over, but
it never really opens up until now. You
see there's a few riders up the road.
So, actually, there is a couple guys
split off, but it's such a big road
where with so many riders, I was going
to be patient. Let's see what happens. I
mean, if it doesn't stay away for a few
minutes, then I don't have to start
chasing them. I can always try to bridge
if they really stay away, and that's
going to be very difficult as well, but
I can put in that effort if I need to.
Right now there's enough riders who are
chasing these these breakaway guys. So
there's no need for me to start doing
that because there's other riders
chasing already. Also remember that
there is a big team with a sprinter. So,
I couldn't see who was in this breakaway
right now, but if it's not a few riders
of this Dubai Police team and they have
their sprinter in the pelaton, who do
you think is going to bring up the speed
and get back this this breakaway riders?
Um, it's going to be the Dubai Police
team, of course, because they want to
lead out their sprinter in the end. And
if there's a breakaway without them,
then they're going to put in the effort.
But, as I'm talking about it, the the
the gap is already closed. It's already
one big long single file line and
there's pretty much no gap anymore. So
every time this was happening, guys
would attack and there's so many guys
chasing it randomly that it in the end
it all forms one long line and then
there's virtually no more gap. Here we
go into another 180 degree turn and I
hit the gas, but then there's a gap and
I I I slide in behind this other rider,
but I didn't really see that he was not
chasing the rest of the rider. So,
there's a massive gap. So, I decide to
to close it since I made the mistake of
uh going behind him. But then the speed
completely uh drops down. This is kind
of the the lull. And there's other
riders who've been driving the pace on
the front the whole time and now they
give up. This is the moment where you
want to attack because all these guys
here, they've put in the effort to try
and break away and now they want to
recover. I'm still fresh. I can go full
gas if I want. So do other riders. So if
you want to attack, this is the moment.
But I wasn't looking to actually create
that breakaway and I was just surfing
wheels. By now it's 20ks to go. I can
see that this is probably going to end
up in a in a mass sprint. So, there's no
use of trying to break away now because
it's just wasting your energy.
So, we're at 78k in the ride. We've got
like 15k to go. And I just happen to be
here where the Dubai police team is uh
is gathering together and chitchatting
probably making some plans about this
sprint finish. Look this guy in the pink
bike who is their sprinter somebody told
me he's talking to all these guys
separately. So they are going to set up
a lead out. That's my expectation. If
you bring a big team like that and one
guy is their sprinter, then they're
going to lead him out and you can take
advantage of that. What I see here is
one big dominant team with a lot of
riders against a whole group of single
riders and a few teams that have only
two or three strong guys. I'm used to
riding like this because I was racing in
Los Angeles against Legion all the time
and they would show up on the criterium
with 10 or 12 riders and they would just
dominate the whole thing by by setting
up a lead out for their sprinter. They
had a a guy guarding in the back and
another guy guarding in the back
creating a big gap after them. So, I'm
going to talk you guys through the last
10 km of this race and how I play this
sprint finish. In the last 10k,
everybody always gets excited,
especially when it's still all together
and then there's so much individual
riders. Look at this, man. What just
happened? One guy overreacts, the other
guy doesn't really react, swears into
me, and we almost crash. These things
are very difficult to avoid and you will
have to sort of trust on uh other riders
skills. What happens a lot is that
people um mist time their their attack
or their sprint or their efforts. So I'm
going to tell you how I do this because
what we're going to see is a long line
of Dubai police riders at the front
driving the pace. What they want to do
is create a high speed so that they
won't get swarmed by other riders and
they keep their sprinter safe. The
faster you go in the front, the harder
it is for other riders to overtake you
or to attack. So, as long as the speed
is super high, nobody will be able to
break away from that. So then they keep
everything together as a pack and as
long as they are in the front going fast
enough, nobody wants to take their wheel
because everybody feels like they're
doing a great job. They're keeping the
pace high and they're drafting behind
them. But what happens is they will be
in the front in a line and then at their
last wheel behind their sprinter, it's
going to be a blob. everybody's going to
fight for that specific wheel because
everybody thinks if I'm in the wheel of
their sprinter, then I'll probably have
a good finish. Well, that's the most
dangerous place to be. So, um not the
most smart place to be. Uh but you do
want to be there at a specific moment in
the race. So, in the top left, you see
the last couple kilometers of this race.
We'll have a few corners and then there
will be a few roundabouts. The day
before I picked up my race number and I
took the car to just drive the last five
kilometers up and back and there's a few
roundabouts on a way more narrow road
towards the finish. So I knew kind I
kind of knew what was coming up and I
knew when I had to be at the front.
That's not the moment right now. We
still have 10k to go. So there's still
plenty of time. You want to draft as
much as you can. You want to time it
right. And patience. Patience is the
word. Patience is super important for a
sprinter. And that's also the most
difficult because you want to stay in
the front because you want to stay safe.
You don't want to be in all those wheels
because crashes are going to happen in
the last couple kilometers. So, it's a
fine line of being either uh more to the
front or more to the side of the pelaton
so you can have ways out and uh being
inside the pelaton to draft as much as
you can. So right now I I I was falling
back a little bit a little bit too much
to my uh preference. So I'm moving up a
little bit. It doesn't take me that much
energy. I'm trying to stay close to the
pelaton now have a good eye on the front
and you can see all these riders of one
team at the front just like I was
expecting. Then there's the Asterero
Falcon guys here in the blue kit. The
sprinter is on my left here on the black
hour. Um, I'm expecting something from
them as well. I was expecting them to
match the lead out of the Dubai Police
at the last moment. They don't have 20
riders available, so they can't do the
same. So, right now they're doing the
same as me and that's trying to draft.
Then there's another few riders here in
the front who are fast and you'll see
them later close to the to the finish.
Um, but I've moved up now quite some
riders and this is about the position
where I want to stay.
We're getting closer to the Dubai Expo
site where the finish will be. So, it
gets more and more jammed up. Everybody
is getting more excited. The the the
riders will be closer together. More
riders are trying to go around just like
this guy here. And you can actually use
riders like that to move up. So, if you
want to move up towards the front, then
you can just hang on to one of those
riders. If they're riding all the way to
the front, you get a free ride up there.
Don't try to do it yourself. I found
this guy that rides to the front and I
stay in his wheel. So, that's my free
ticket towards the front. Again, try to
find riders like this. There's always
riders that will push towards the front.
And you can see Dubai police long single
file line. They still have like 15
riders in the front and they're already
burning off riders. But those riders,
they fall back so fast, which I think is
really weird. Just go all the way to the
side at least. But he falls straight
through the pelaton. It's a bit
dangerous. So maybe they can work on
that a little bit. 15 riders at the
front driving the pace. Right now is the
moment you need to pay attention. You
don't want to fall back anymore because
you can go backwards so fast. within 10
seconds, you can be 50 spots back. So,
very important to keep an eye on what's
going on and where the race is going
towards. So, if it's a right turn, you
want to be on the right side of the
road. If it's a left turn, you want to
be on the left side of the road because
the inside usually is faster. Roundabout
here, a really, really big roundabout in
this case. 45k an hour over the
roundabout, easy. And uh we're
accelerating again. Speeds are going to
ramp up. Remember these guys at the
front have a lot of riders to burn off.
So they basically all take like a couple
hundred meter sprint to keep up the
speed as high as they can. So pushing
hard here to stay with the pack and I
don't want to fall back anymore. If
you're not moving up, you're moving
back. That's how it goes. On the left
here is start finish. We've got 2k out,
2k back. We're going under a tunnel
right now. So the this is going
downhill. The sprint, the last 200
meters will be uphill. One thing to
remember, and you can see it's getting
more chaotic. It's getting more crowded
right now. Everybody wants to be at the
front. Everybody is chasing that last
wheel of that spring train, like I said,
and that's the place where you want to
be, but it's the most dangerous spot to
be. So, I'm going to time it right. I
don't want to be there now. I want to be
there when it's time. Trying to defend
my spot. As I said, try to move up. So
here I have a little bit of a gap. So I
move up, overtake five, six riders, and
then we go over this roundabout. Check
out the rider in blue here. I don't know
what he does, but he burps his rear tire
causing kind of a bit of a a mess. We
are slowing down, having to to to push
hard again. Another Dubai police rider
is there, burned off, and we've got some
some speed bumps in this last couple K.
This was a really cool sprint finish,
and it's going fast. 54k an hour. So,
decent pace. Very nice job from these
guys at the front. At this moment, I'm
using my momentum because I wasn't
pushing that hard just to move up a few
riders. And I want to stay on the side a
little bit because that gives me
options. If I need to, I can go around
to the left, go to the front, but I can
also draft a little bit of the rider in
front of me. If I would be more to the
right in the back, I would have nowhere
to go. I can't react. But now I can.
Usually it doesn't take too long for
other riders to go around you. Here
there's a guy in blue. He goes next to
me and he just starts riding into me. I
don't know what he was thinking. I have
nowhere to go. This guy is just bumping
shoulders. And here I'm trying to
persuade him to go to the front. There's
a roundabout coming up.
He doesn't really respond. I know what I
need to do. I am going to move up and
defend my position. If you let this
inside open, other guys will swer around
you. And then you go from 10th till 20th
spot, just like that. But now I guard my
position on the inside of this
roundabout and I can sort of aim where I
want to be. Now I misjudged it a little
bit because I actually thought this
roundabout would be straight and the
next one would be the 180. So now I end
up all the way in the front here, which
I don't want. We've got two kilometers
to go. So, I move to the side, let other
guys move up, and then now I need to
find a spot back into the train. Now,
this is a nice spot. Guy in blue is next
to me, and I'm just trying to stay next
to him. I'm not pushing into him. I'm
respecting his position, and then
usually it doesn't take long until other
guys would come around me. Here's
another Dubai police guy. Maybe he's
going to reinforce the sprint lead out.
And I'm just matching whatever these
guys are doing, trying to get a little
bit of draft. The last couple corners
will be to the right. And right there,
there's another guy that I can draft
off. This is a good spot. We've got
couple hundred meters to go. And there's
still a few girls in the leadout train
of the Dubai police. Now, with all
respect, I don't think they should be
here right now. Uh it it makes it
dangerous. They are probably riding for
their first position or they're actually
the the the blockers of their sprinter.
So if they are on the wheel of their
sprinter then other riders like me can't
be on their wheel. It's a strategy.
Another Dubai police guy here is pulling
off. He's done his job. So nice job by
that team. I think they still have two
guys in front of their sprinter. So
they're going to launch their sprint to
lead him out. And then there's three or
four riders in front of me. So I think I
still have a good position. I need to
wait. I need to be patient. I want to
launch my sprint with about 200 to go
just as we come underneath that bridge.
That's my plan right now. And the only
thing I need to work with is this girl
here on my right because if she's going
to be there, she's going to be blocking
my sprint. Luckily, she gives me some
space as we come over this roundabout
and I don't have to force myself into
it. Now, behind me, you can see it's all
single file, so it's nice and fast and
in full focus. Right now, we got 500 m
to go. On my left side, there's the guy
from Astero Falcon. It's their sprinter.
He's trying to slide in. That's okay
with me because I know what he's going
to do. He's going to launch it. We've
got 300 meters to go. I'm getting ready
for the sprint. I'm standing up. I've
got high cadence. And now he's going.
So, I'm going in his wheel. He's
practically giving me a lead out. We're
already overtaking one, two, three, four
guys. The Moroccan guy here on my right
side. Very nice sprint position. Full
gas. I'm trying to get out of his wheel
to go around, but I couldn't make it
happen. So back in the slipstream and I
finished third in the spinnies 92 UCI
Grand Fondo of Dubai and with the third
place overall in the elite category I
won my biggest cash prize to date. Also
I took the first in the 35 plus age
group which meant a win in the UCI grand
fondo and my very first UCI winners
jersey. So guys please remember fancy
bike is not your priority. It's what you
do with the bike that makes the
difference. Don't forget to check those
discount links down below. You'll also
support the channel by using them. I've
got some gravel races coming up, so
please subscribe and I'll see you in the
next video. See you.
videos.curation_reason: High-quality first-person race recap with detailed bike setup analysis and winning performance perspective.

videos.title: Spinneys Dubai 92 Cycling Challenge | Build-up Ride 1 | Cycling | The JY Project
videos.description:
Open to all levels of cyclists, our regular Build-Up Rides at the Al Qudra Cycle Track will help you increase your speed and stamina in the months leading up to the main event. Enter our Aster Pharmacy BIG 5 competition as a team or an individual to secure all 5 races (4 Build-Up Rides and the 92km Main Event) and receive these great perks:

BUILD-UP RIDE DATES
Build-Up Ride 1: 35km – Sunday, 1 October 2023
Build-Up Ride 2: 45km – Sunday, 29 October 2023
Build-Up Ride 3: 65km – Sunday, 3 December 2023
Build-Up Ride 4: 85km – Sunday, 28 January 2024
Spinneys Dubai 92 Cycle Challenge Main Event (UCI Dubai Gran Fondo): 92km – Sunday, 25 February 2024

For more details, please visit their website at https://cyclechallenge.ae/
#spinneyscyclingchallenge
#dubai92
#cyclinguae
#uaecycling
#uae #dubai 
#alqudracycling
#thejyproject

videos.title: Spinneys 92 Cycle Race 2026
videos.description:
Welcome to one of the UAE’s biggest cycling events of the year — the Spinneys Dubai 92 Cycle Challenge.

Thousands of riders. Closed roads. 92KM of pure endurance.  From elite racers chasing qualification spots to everyday athletes pushing personal limits — this is Dubai cycling at its absolute best.

As an official UCI Gran Fondo World Series Qualifier, today’s race isn’t just about the finish line… it’s about earning a place on the world stage. Expect fast packs, breakaways, sprint finishes, and an electric atmosphere from start to finish.

Event Details
📍 Expo City Dubai
📅 Sunday, February 15, 2026
🚴 92KM Closed-Road Course

Subscribe for more LIVE UAE sports coverage, cycling events, and endurance action.
👍 Like the stream
💬 Comment where you’re watching from
🔔 Turn notifications on


Follow FFP Sports:
Instagram: @ffpsports
YouTube: FFP Sports
Website: www.ffpsports.com

quotes.text: Dubai actually has a whole network of about 200 km of dedicated cycling path. Is it straight and flat and boring? Well, I'm used to that back home and the weather was so much better.
28 mm is wide enough on these silky smooth roads of Dubai. In this case, wider in my opinion would not be faster, and I was actually running higher pressure than usual because it was so smooth.

terrain_notes.text: Dubai has silky smooth roads where 28mm tires are wide enough and higher pressure than usual can be run effectively.

gear_mentions.text: 28mm tires are wide enough on the silky smooth roads of Dubai - wider would not be faster in this case.
Swap compact crankset (34/50) for 39/53 to handle the high speeds in Dubai - the compact is way too small for this race.
Use steel straight spoke wheels like Yolio Pro Next because they are easy to replace in local bike shops if damaged during air travel or crashes.
Choose Vittoria Corsa over Corsa Pro tires for better durability and puncture resistance - a few watts savings never make up for a 3-hour walk in the desert when tires fail.

race_day_tips.text: Hot wax your chain with degreasing additive for Dubai's dry and sandy conditions - claims are up to 1000km without reapplying.

additional_quotes.text: Dubai actually has a whole network of about 200km of dedicated cycling path. We stay in town square right next to the start of the Aludra cycling track.
Dubai 92 cycling challenge comes with five events - four build up rides and the main event. Build up rides are 35km, 45km, 65km, and 85km leading to the main 92km event.

rider_intel.search_text: The Spinneys Dubai 92 Cycle Challenge features silky smooth roads where riders can run 28mm tires at higher pressure than usual. The course consists of fast, flat terrain that demands bigger gears - riders recommend swapping compact cranksets for 39/53 to handle high speeds. The race is part of a series with four build-up rides of 35km, 45km, 65km, and 85km leading to the main 92km event. Dubai offers 200km of dedicated cycling paths, with the Aludra cycling track starting near town square. The dry and sandy conditions favor hot-waxed chains for durability. Gear reliability is crucial due to the desert environment - steel spoke wheels and puncture-resistant tires are recommended over marginal performance gains.
